73769.subject: Claude Opus 4.8: The Complete Prompting Playbook for 2026 🧠
73769.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/claude-opus-4-8-prompting-playbook

👋 Hey, Linas here! Every day, I break down 3 stories shaping the future of FinTech & Artificial Intelligence - plus the money movements and trends worth tracking. First time here? 387k+ FinTech and AI leaders get this daily. Join them:
On May 28, Anthropic announced a $65 billion Series H round at a whopping $965 billion post-money valuation, making it the most valuable private (AI) company on Earth. The same day, it released Claude Opus 4.8.
That timing is definitely intentional here. Opus 4.8 is the model Anthropic is betting its near-trillion-dollar story on.
Anthropic describes it as an upgrade on Opus 4.7 [ https://substack.com/redirect/9bedfb03-ab75-4fe6-a827-17b4f3c2ba3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] “with improvements across benchmarks” and “a more effective collaborator,” available at the same price ($5/$25 per million input/output tokens). The framing is clear as Opus 4.8 builds on 4.7’s foundation rather than breaking from it.
The prompting instincts you developed with 4.7 [ https://substack.com/redirect/9bedfb03-ab75-4fe6-a827-17b4f3c2ba3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] still apply. But there are meaningful new capabilities, and one genuinely new paradigm, that change what’s achievable if you know how to use them.
The biggest one is undoubtedly Dynamic Workflows in Claude Code: the ability for Claude to write its own orchestration scripts, spin up parallel subagents, and scale test-time compute trivially. Anthropic engineers have been using this as a daily driver for months. The rest of the world found out yesterday.
To make this as easy and seamless as possible, we’re also sharing Opus 4.8 Prompt Optimizer, a custom Claude skill [ https://substack.com/redirect/482b5034-3d89-4fff-a0a6-e64e4fa1ee2b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] that rewrites any prompt using the framework below automatically. Step-by-step setup instructions are at the end.
If you’ve read the Claude Opus 4.7 Prompting Playbook [ https://substack.com/redirect/9bedfb03-ab75-4fe6-a827-17b4f3c2ba3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], the good news is that your existing prompts still work. But the new capabilities reward a specific set of upgrades, particularly around the effort stack, honesty directives, and Dynamic Workflows. This guide covers all of it.
Below is everything you need to get the best output this model can produce, every single time.
Step 0 - Choose the Right Model
Before writing a single word of your prompt, choose the right model. Anthropic’s current lineup includes Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and Claude Haiku 4.5, each optimized for different workloads.
→ Claude Opus 4.8 is now the flagship. It scores 69.2% on SWE-bench Pro, 88.6% on SWE-bench Verified, and 1890 Elo on GDPval-AA (121 Elo points ahead

CMC Market Pulse:
The Essential Weekly Crypto Briefing

Welcome to this week’s CMC Market Pulse newsletter!

CMC Market Pulse delivers actionable market insights, giving you
all the key narratives and developments you need in under 5
minutes.

Let's dive into this week.

Market Overview 📈
-----------------

Bitcoin (BTC) collapsed further this week, falling another
4.74%,
while Ethereum (ETH) fell further, dropping
5.53%
in the same week. Total crypto market cap suffered as well,
falling
0.62%
to
$2.47T,
down from $2.58T from the previous week.

Weekly liquidations saw a large increase on the long side, with
the crypto market in general taking a hit over the week. Long
liquidations hit a high of $654M on Thursday, almost double the
usual values. Funding rates interestingly show no decline, with
funding rates across majors rising instead, perhaps indicating an
optimistic stance.

* Michael Saylor’s Strategy acquires no Bitcoin over the past
week, repurchasing $1.5B in their 2029 convertible notes instead.
* MasterCard secures the New York State Department of Financial
Services BitLicense to support stablecoin and digital payment
infrastructure.
* Grayscale delays plans for its IPO, citing weak market
conditions, with plans unlikely to restart before Q4 2026.
The week kicked off strong with equities, with the move
following through after the Memorial Day weekend. While equities
soared, crypto far

73755.subject: $1.1B Stolen in DeFi. Institutions Still Want In
73755.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

These were our top news today:

* **[Crypto](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/crypto-liquidations-near-1-billion-24-hours-g13uhz)**[ liquidations near $1B in 24 hours as leverage unwinds](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/crypto-liquidations-near-1-billion-24-hours-g13uhz)

* **[Base](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/base-launches-azul-upgrade-takes-step-toward-stage-2-decentralization)**[ hits Stage 2 with multiproofs, 99% fewer empty blocks](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/base-launches-azul-upgrade-takes-step-toward-stage-2-decentralization)

* **[Vitalik](https://thedefiant.io/news/people/vitalik-buterin-endorses-interfold-privacy-protocol-voting-auctions-m4ldfh)**[ endorses Interfold, ZK+FHE privacy stack for voting and auctions](https://thedefiant.io/news/people/vitalik-buterin-endorses-interfold-privacy-protocol-voting-auctions-m4ldfh)

* **[Tempo L1](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/tempo-l1-hits-3-9m-transactions-in-two-months)**[ hits 3.9M transactions in two months, $25M+ stablecoin supply](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/tempo-l1-hits-3-9m-transactions-in-two-months)

It was a bit of a slow news day, but the standout for me was the [livestream discussion](https://x.com/i/broadcasts/1jxXggbEnMyJZ) we held on @DefiantNews X this morning: Are Institutions still coming to DeFi after the latest string of hacks?

As we [reported yesterday](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/openzeppelin-pushes-back-after-ex-cto-declares-all-of-defi-unsafe), Manuel Araoz, the co-founder of OpenZeppelin, the security shop which reviewed Aave, Compound, and Maker, posted that he now considers **"all of DeFi unsafe,"** arguing that AI coding agents have turned vulnerability-hunting into a game defenders structurally can't win. The backdrop is ugly: **roughly $630M drained across 27 exploits in April alone, $1.1B over twelve months, KelpDAO down $292M to Lazarus, Drift down $285M.**

So I sat down with John Zettler of Kraken, Sun Raghupathi of Veda, and Anthony DeMartino of Sentora, three people whose entire business is convincing institutions to route capital into this stuff, and asked: Are your phones ringing with panic, or worse, is it just crickets?

**The opposite, it turns out.**

"My phone has been blowing up since our launch," Zettler said of Kraken's new Bitcoin vault, which crossed $60M in TVL within a day. Sentora's DeMartino said his firm "landed three deals in the last five weeks," during the worst stretch of hacks, when he'd expected a pullback. **"The conversations have heated up, not slowed down since the incidents in April. The coincidence is hard to explain."**

Here's my attempt to explain it: **The hacks aren't deterring institutions because they changed what kind of risk DeFi is.**

As Sun put it, **"risk is now moving from the smart contract layer into the operational layer."** Almost none of the recent disasters were broken math in a contract. They were compromised keys,

73274.subject: Svadobná výzdoba
73274.body:
Zdravíme nevestu💍🤍,

Ďakujeme veľmi pekne za vašu správu.. Termín svadby máme ešte voľný a veľmi
radi budeme súčasťou vašej svadby.🤍

Spravidla sa s klientami stretávame osobne v showroome v Rozhanovciach
(kúsok od Košíc) kde si spolu vieme vyskladať svadobnú výzdobu a na základe
toho vytvoriť cenovú ponuku na mieru pre vás ak by ste mali záujem prosím
navrhnite deň a čas kedy by vám to najviac vyhovovalo aby sa časovo radi
prispôsobíme vášmu času. Poprípade, ak sme mimo Košíc či Prešova, radi si s
Vami dohodneme videohovor. Prosím navrhnite deň a čas, ktorý by Vám
vyhovoval najviac, pokojne aj cez víkend aj vo večerných hodinách. 🙂
Najbližsie voľne termíny máme ešte aj tento týždeň. Forme stretnutia sa
radi prispôsobíme Vám, čo je pre Vás praktickejšie ☺️

Budeme sa tešiť na prípadnú spoluprácu s Vami.

S pozdravom

Lenka Gattiker,
Vaša osobná posh asistentka

73231.subject: From a Cornell Lab to BlackRock. The Avalanche Story. | One Block Weekly #11
73231.body:
Hello,

Welcome back to One Block Weekly. One coin, one story.

While most blockchains were still arguing about speed, one quietly went and built a different kind of network altogether.

🏔️ Avalanche (AVAX)

Emin Gun Sirer is a Cornell University computer science professor who had been researching distributed systems long before most people knew what a blockchain was.

In 2018, his team published a whitepaper introducing a new consensus mechanism, a way for a network to agree on what is true, that was faster and more scalable than anything Bitcoin or Ethereum used. They called it Avalanche.

Ava Labs was founded to build a blockchain around it. Avalanche launched its mainnet in September 2020, raised $230 million in a public token sale in 48 hours, and immediately positioned itself as the platform serious institutions would eventually choose.

The pitch was simple: all the programmability of Ethereum, none of the congestion.

⚡ A Few Moments That Shaped Avalanche

2021 - AVAX surged over 2,700% in a single year, hitting an all-time high of around $147. Developers flooded in, and Avalanche briefly became the fourth largest blockchain by total value locked.

2022 - Avalanche partnered with Deloitte to build a disaster relief reimbursement platform for US government agencies. A blockchain landing a Big Four accounting firm and a government contract in the same deal was a first.

2023 - The subnet model gained traction. Major institutions, including financial firms and gaming companies, began launching their own custom blockchains, called subnets, built on top of Avalanche infrastructure.

2024 - BlackRock and other asset managers began tokenising real-world assets on Avalanche, bringing government bonds and funds onto the blockchain. Institutional adoption had moved from talk to action.

🔢 What Makes It Different

Avalanche finalises transactions in under two seconds, faster than almost any major blockchain. But the more interesting feature is subnets: custom blockchains that can be spun up within the Avalanche ecosystem, each with its own rules, validators, and token economics.

For institutions that want the benefits of blockchain without sharing space with memecoins and DeFi protocols, that is a compelling offer.

🤯 Three Weird Avalanche Facts

The founder once publicly exposed a flaw in a rival blockchain. Emin Gun Sirer identified and disclosed a critical vulnerability in Bitcoin's network years before Avalanche existed. He is one of the few people in crypto who arrived with an academic reputation already intact.

A major gaming subnet launched and went quiet. One of the highest profile early subnets was built for a blockchain gaming ecosystem. It launched with significant fanfare and struggled to find its audience, becoming an early lesson in the gap between institutional infrastructure and consumer adoption.

The whitepaper was published anonymously. The original Avalanche consensus paper was released und
